issues.summary: This work item will elaborate and refine the Jira issue description so it is clear, structured, and actionable. Transform a brief or incomplete summary into a detailled description that communicates the problem, context, goals, and expected outcomes.
issues.description:
Summary
This work item aims to elaborate and refine the existing summary section of the Jira description so that it clearly communicates the purpose, context, and intended outcomes of the task. The focus is on transforming a brief or incomplete summary into a well-structured, professional, and actionable description that can be understood quickly by all stakeholders.
The enhanced summary will correct any typographical errors, clarify ambiguous phrasing, and ensure that key details about the work are captured. Where concrete information is not yet available, structured placeholders are provided so the team can efficiently complete the description without reworking the format.
By improving the clarity and completeness of the summary, this task supports better alignment among contributors, smoother handoffs, and more accurate planning and estimation.
Background
Describe the context that led to this work item, including any prior decisions, related initiatives, or problems with the current summary that prompted this improvement.
Objective
Clearly state what this work item must achieve in terms of the updated summary (for example, making it unambiguous, aligned with project goals, and immediately actionable for the delivery team).
Scope
Outline what aspects of the Jira description are in scope for refinement, such as the main summary line, supporting paragraphs, and any structured sections that clarify requirements or expectations.
Out of scope
Specify any work that will not be addressed as part of this item, such as implementing the underlying feature, updating technical documentation, or changing acceptance criteria owned by another team.
Success criteria
Define how success will be measured for the revised summary, for example: stakeholders can understand the intent of the work without additional explanation, the summary is free from errors, and it provides enough context for planning and prioritisation.
Risks and dependencies
Capture any risks or dependencies that cou

issues.summary: get first github item (branch, commit, pr, etc.) using jira wrk item # on github too reference it and then appear on jira/attlassian confirming successful integration.
issues.description:
Objective
Integrate Jira with GitHub to retrieve the first GitHub item associated with a Jira work item.
Context
This issue aims to establish a connection between Jira and GitHub. The goal is to reference GitHub items like branches, commits, or pull requests using Jira work item numbers.
Scope
Included:
Fetching the first GitHub item related to a Jira work item.
Ensuring the integration displays confirmation in Jira/Atlassian.
Not Included:
Additional features beyond the first item retrieval.
Any unrelated integrations.
Success criteria
Successful retrieval of the first GitHub item (branch, commit, PR).
Confirmation of integration visible in Jira/Atlassian.
Accurate referencing of GitHub items using Jira work item numbers.
Risks and dependencies
Potential issues with API connectivity between Jira and GitHub.
Dependency on the correct configuration of both Jira and GitHub accounts.
Other information
N/A

issues.summary: Catch quit failures before reinitializing Valkey client
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/163
Problem
When the singleton guard detects an unhealthy existing client, it calls shutdownValkey() directly inside the catch path. If client.quit() rejects (common when the socket is already broken), that rejection escapes createValkeyClient() and prevents the intended reinitialization/fallback path from running.
This means a transient Redis failure during a second initialization attempt can bubble up as a hard failure instead of recovering gracefully.
Steps to Reproduce
Initialize Valkey client successfully
Break the socket/connection
Trigger a second call to createValkeyClient()
Observe the rejection from client.quit() escapes instead of falling through to reinit
Expected Behavior
quit() failures should be caught within the shutdown path so that createValkeyClient() can proceed with reinitialization/fallback.
Acceptance Criteria
client.quit() rejections are caught inside the shutdown/catch path
createValkeyClient() successfully reinitializes after a transient connection failure
Unit test covers the quit-failure + reinit scenario

issues.summary: VALKEY_HOST unset after prior init causes stale client handle leak in createValkeyClient()
issues.description:
GitHub Issue: https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/162
Problem
The singleton guard docstring says an existing healthy client is returned immediately, but the function still returns null early when VALKEY_HOST is unset. If createValkeyClient() was previously called with VALKEY_HOST set, then later calls with VALKEY_HOST removed will return null while leaving the existing client (and any refresh timer) running.
This is inconsistent with the docs and can leak handles in tests/dev reloads.
Expected Behavior
Either:
Check/clean up the existing client before the VALKEY_HOST early-return, OR
Update the docs to explicitly describe this behavior
Acceptance Criteria
No handle/timer leaks when VALKEY_HOST is unset after a prior successful init
Behavior matches documentation
Test covers the VALKEY_HOST-removed-after-init scenario

issues.summary: Fix stack trace / exception detail exposure in Flask API responses
issues.description:
Summary
CodeQL (py/stack-trace-exposure) flagged multiple locations where internal exception details, error messages, and stack traces are returned directly to API clients. This can expose internal infrastructure details (model names, file paths, error types) to end users.
Affected locations
blueprints/api_bp.py:133, 135, 141, 158, 161, 180, 199
blueprints/auth_api_bp.py:105, 219
blueprints/generation_bp.py:312
Example (generation_bp.py:312)
return (f"Sorry, there was an error generating the recipe. Details: {last_error_message}", 500)last_error_message contains exception type names, model names, and internal error strings that should never reach the client.
Acceptance Criteria
All API error responses return generic user-facing messages (e.g. "An internal error occurred")
Internal exception details are logged server-side only (not returned in response body)
CodeQL py/stack-trace-exposure alerts resolved on next scan
Applies to both JSON API endpoints and legacy HTML form endpoints

issues.summary: Add Express-layer request validation with body buffering (v1.1.0)
issues.description:
Context
server/validation.ts was removed in v1.0.0 because it was dead code — express-validator requires a parsed request body, but the Express proxy (createFlaskProxy) must mount before express.json() so the raw body stream can be piped to Flask. Once the body is consumed by any Express middleware, Flask receives an empty request.
Flask already validates all AI endpoints (validate_generation_input() in generation_api_bp.py), so there is no security gap in v1.0.0.
Additionally, the removed validateImageRequest was checking for recipeName + keywords in the body, but the actual endpoint is POST /api/generate_image/<recipe_id> — a mismatched contract that could never have been wired correctly.
What needs to be done
Design and implement a proper Express validation layer that works with the streaming proxy. Options to evaluate:
Body-buffer approach — Read the raw body as a Buffer before the proxy, validate, then reconstruct a readable stream for the proxy to pipe to Flask.
http-proxy-middleware with fixRequestBody — Replace the custom proxy.ts with http-proxy-middleware, which supports body-parser integration via fixRequestBody.
Validation at the edge only — Use HTTP header or query-param validation at Express (Content-Type checks, size limits) and keep body validation in Flask.
Acceptance criteria
POST /api/generate validates prompt: string, 1–500 characters, at Express layer before the request hits Flask
POST /api/generate_image/<id> validates route param format at Express layer
Body is correctly forwarded to Flask (Flask receives the same payload the browser sent)
No regression on auth, recipe, or collections endpoints
Unit tests cover the validation middleware
Notes
The current proxy.ts uses req.pipe(proxyReq) — any solution must preserve raw streaming for non-AI routes
See ADR for the original proxy design decision (Express single-origin pattern)

issues.summary: CI/CD: Split into pr-gate.yml + release.yml — auto trigger on dev→main PR
issues.description:
Goal

Replace the keyword-based gcbuildanddeploy trigger with a fully automatic two-workflow CI/CD pipeline.

## Current state

gc-build-deploy.yml only fires when commit messages or PR body contain gcbuild/gcdeploy/gcbuildanddeploy keywords. Manual, error-prone, not enforced.

## Target

**pr-gate.yml** — fires on pull_request targeting main from any dev* branch:
- Frontend: lint, type-check, test, build
- Backend: pytest via submodule
- CHANGELOG validation
- Coverage threshold enforcement
- All jobs are required status checks (branch protection)

**release.yml** — fires on push to main (i.e. after PR merge):
- Extract version from package.json
- Create git tag v{version}
- Extract CHANGELOG excerpt
- Create GitHub Release
- Submit Cloud Build with version substitution
- Post deploy result as PR comment

## Acceptance criteria

- Opening a PR from dev to main automatically triggers all quality gates
- Merging the PR automatically creates a tagged GitHub Release and deploys to Cloud Run
- No manual keyword needed
- Branch protection on main requires pr-gate status checks to pass before merge is allowed

issues.summary: CI/CD: Add Backend pytest to PR gate — Flask submodule must pass before merge
issues.description:
Goal

Run pytest against the Backend submodule as a required gate on every PR to main.

## Current state

backend-submodule-audit in gc-build-deploy.yml only checks that the Backend/ directory exists and is non-empty. No Python tests run. A broken Flask change can merge to main undetected.

## Implementation

Add a backend-ci job to pr-gate.yml:

yaml
backend-ci:
  name: Backend Python tests
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        submodules: recursive
    - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with:
        python-version: '3.12'
    - name: Install uv
      run: pip install uv
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: cd Backend && uv sync
    - name: Run pytest
      run: cd Backend && uv run pytest --tb=short
    - name: Upload coverage
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: backend-coverage
        path: Backend/htmlcov/


## Acceptance criteria

- pytest runs on every PR to main
- Failing tests block merge
- Coverage report uploaded as artifact

issues.summary: CI/CD: Auto SemVer tagging + GitHub Release on merge to main
issues.description:
Goal

Every merge to main automatically creates a SemVer git tag and a GitHub Release, so deployed images are always traceable to an exact version.

## Current state

Images are tagged only with $SHORT_SHA. No v1.0.0 tag exists. You cannot determine what version is running in Cloud Run from the image tag alone.

## Implementation in release.yml

yaml
- name: Extract version
  id: version
  run: echo "version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

- name: Create git tag
  run: |
    git tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    git push origin v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}

- name: Extract changelog excerpt
  id: changelog
  run: |
    # Extract section between current version header and previous
    ...

- name: Create GitHub Release
  uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
  with:
    tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
    body: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.excerpt }}
    draft: false
    prerelease: false


## Acceptance criteria

- Every merge to main produces a v{major}.{minor}.{patch} git tag
- A GitHub Release is created with CHANGELOG excerpt as body
- Tag collision (same version merged twice) is detected and fails gracefully
- package.json version bump is required as part of the PR process

issues.summary: CI/CD: Set FLASK_ENV=production in cloudbuild.yaml Flask deploy step
issues.description:
Goal

Add FLASK_ENV=production to the --set-env-vars flag in the Flask Cloud Run deploy step so that production-only guards introduced in v1.0.0 actually activate.

## Why this is critical

Two security fixes added in v1.0.0 check os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV") == "production":

1. CRITICAL-2 — OAUTHLIB_INSECURE_TRANSPORT guard: without FLASK_ENV=production, the insecure transport flag remains set in Cloud Run
2. CRITICAL-4 — FLASK_SECRET_KEY fail-fast: without FLASK_ENV=production, a missing secret key silently generates an ephemeral key instead of crashing at startup

## Fix

In cloudbuild.yaml step Deploy Flask Backend, add to --set-env-vars:


FLASK_ENV=production


## Acceptance criteria

- FLASK_ENV=production is present in the Flask Cloud Run service env vars after deploy
- OAUTHLIB_INSECURE_TRANSPORT is NOT set in the Cloud Run environment
- Missing FLASK_SECRET_KEY causes deployment startup failure (fast-fail)
- Verified via gcloud run services describe flask-backend --format=json | jq '.spec.template.spec.containers[].env'

issues.summary: CI/CD: Fix deploy ordering — Flask backend must fully deploy before Express frontend
issues.description:
Goal

Ensure Flask backend is fully deployed and serving before the Express frontend is updated, eliminating the rollout window where new frontend code hits old Flask API.

## Current state

In cloudbuild.yaml:
- Deploy Express Frontend waits on Push Express Frontend Image (its own push)
- Deploy Flask Backend waits on Push Flask Backend Image (its own push)
- Both deploys run in parallel — for 30-60 seconds, new Angular code can be live while old Flask handles /api/generate

## Fix

Change Deploy Express Frontend to also wait on Deploy Flask Backend:

yaml
- name: 'Deploy Express Frontend'
  id: 'Deploy Express Frontend'
  waitFor: ['Push Express Frontend Image', 'Deploy Flask Backend']  # ← add this


This adds ~60s to total deploy time but eliminates version skew.

## Acceptance criteria

- Express frontend Cloud Run revision goes live only after Flask backend revision is healthy
- Total build+deploy pipeline time is acceptable (target < 8 minutes end-to-end)
- Verified by checking Cloud Run revision timestamps in GCP console after a deploy

issues.summary: CI/CD: Fix ci.yml branch targets (develop → dev) + add coverage threshold
issues.description:
Goal

Fix ci.yml to target the actual integration branch name and enforce a minimum test coverage threshold.

## Issues

1. ci.yml has branches: [main, develop] — the integration branch is dev (or dev/*), not develop. CI never runs on feature PRs targeting dev.
2. Tests run and upload a coverage report but there is no enforced minimum. Coverage can silently drop to 0%.

## Fixes

### Branch targets
yaml
on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev, 'dev/**']
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, dev]


### Coverage threshold (Vitest)
In vitest.config.ts or package.json:
json
"coverage": {
  "thresholds": {
    "lines": 70,
    "functions": 70,
    "branches": 60
  }
}


Or enforce in the workflow step:
bash
npm run test:ci -- --coverage.thresholds.lines=70


## Acceptance criteria

- CI runs on PRs targeting dev (not just main)
- Build fails if coverage drops below threshold
- Threshold values agreed and documented

issues.summary: CI/CD: Tag Docker images with SemVer alongside SHORT_SHA in cloudbuild.yaml
issues.description:
Goal

Tag every production Docker image with both $SHORT_SHA (for traceability) and the SemVer version (e.g. v1.0.0) so images can be pulled by version and rollbacks are unambiguous.

## Current state

Images are only tagged $SHORT_SHA. You cannot docker pull flask-backend:v1.0.0.

## Implementation

In cloudbuild.yaml, after pushing $SHORT_SHA images, add a re-tag step:

yaml
- name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
  id: 'Tag Express Frontend with version'
  waitFor: ['Push Express Frontend Image']
  args:
    - tag
    - $_IMAGE_REGISTRY/express-frontend:$SHORT_SHA
    - $_IMAGE_REGISTRY/express-frontend:$_VERSION

- name: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
  id: 'Push Express Frontend Version Tag'
  waitFor: ['Tag Express Frontend with version']
  args: [push, '$_IMAGE_REGISTRY/express-frontend:$_VERSION']


The _VERSION substitution is passed from release.yml when it calls gcloud builds submit --substitutions=_VERSION=v1.0.0,...

## Acceptance criteria

- Both flask-backend:v1.0.0 and flask-backend:abc1234 exist in Artifact Registry after deploy
- latest tag also updated on each release
- Rollback procedure documented: gcloud run deploy --image=.../flask-backend:v0.9.0

issues.summary: CI/CD: Add CHANGELOG.md validation gate on dev→main PR
issues.description:
Goal

Block merges to main if CHANGELOG.md has not been updated in the PR, ensuring every release has documented changes.

## Implementation

Add a step to pr-gate.yml:

yaml
- name: Validate CHANGELOG updated
  run: |
    if ! git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD | grep -q 'CHANGELOG.md'; then
      echo "❌ CHANGELOG.md must be updated before merging to main"
      echo "Add an entry under the [Unreleased] section or create a new version section"
      exit 1
    fi
    # Also verify the version in package.json has a matching section in CHANGELOG
    VERSION=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")
    if ! grep -q "## \[$VERSION\]" CHANGELOG.md && ! grep -q '## \[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md; then
      echo "❌ No matching version section or [Unreleased] section found in CHANGELOG.md"
      exit 1
    fi
    echo "✅ CHANGELOG.md updated"


## Acceptance criteria

- PR to main that does not touch CHANGELOG.md fails the gate
- CHANGELOG format follows Keep a Changelog convention
- [Unreleased] section is acceptable (release.yml converts it to versioned section on merge)

issues.summary: [repo-status] Daily Status Report — April 20, 2026 🌱
issues.description:
🌟 Vegangenius Chef — Daily Status
Date: April 20, 2026 · Branch: dev → main
🚀 Highlights
A fresh bug surfaced today on the auth front, and the agentic tooling layer is richer than ever. The codebase is in good shape with two actionable PRs waiting for review. Let's keep the momentum! 💪
🐛 New Issue Today
#2858 — Auth UI Mismatch ()
A session-state bug was filed this morning: recipes appear in authenticated mode while the UI still shows the "Login" button. This happens on browser re-open when a prior session exists.
Root cause area: mismatch between the persisted session/token and the Angular AuthService signal state on startup
Priority: 🔴 High — visible to returning users; damages trust in the login flow
Suggested approach: ensure AuthService calls /api/auth/check on app init and properly reflects the session result in the auth signal before PersistenceService loads recipes
📋 Open PRs
PR 
Title 
Status 
#2856 
Use npm-installed Copilot CLI in Daily Repo Status workflow 
🔵 Draft — needs promotion + review 
#2843 
fix(TAS-8): decouple image_service from Flask request context 
🟡 Open — 99/99 tests passing, ready to merge

issues.summary: [repo-status] Daily Status Report — April 21, 2026 🌱
issues.description:
🌟 Vegangenius Chef — Daily Status
Date: April 21, 2026 · Branch: dev
🚀 Big Wins Since Yesterday
Yesterday was an absolute powerhouse of shipping! The team landed two major architecture upgrades that dramatically improve scalability and public discoverability.
⚡ Phase 4: Pub/Sub Async AI Generation (merged #2869 + #2870)
The app now generates recipes and images asynchronously — no more blocking HTTP calls! Highlights:
/api/generate returns HTTP 202 immediately, with generation happening in the background
Pub/Sub workers migrated to Cloud Run HTTP Push endpoints (/api/worker/recipe, /api/worker/image) — true serverless scale-to-zero, no 24/7 background worker needed
Angular frontend updated with a polling service to pick up results
Recipe database model extended with a status field to track generation progress
This is a huge reliability and cost win. 🎉
🔐 Security Fix: Google Cloud Scope (commits e9d8c76, bd11b6b)
Removed an overly broad cloud-platform scope from the user OAuth flow — a good least-privilege tightening that closes a potential privilege escalation path.
🔧 Submodule Plumbing Fixed (PR #2867)
alirez-claude-skills and gemstack are now properly registered in .gitmodules with correct URLs. git submodule update --init alirez-claude-skills gemstack will now work for all contributors.
🆕 Hot Off the Press — Opened Today
#2871 — feat(ssr): SSR Routes for /r/*, /browse, /sitemap.xml
This PR mounts SSR proxy routes in Express before the Angular catch-all, so crawlers get server-rendered HTML from Flask instead of the empty SPA shell. Also bumps Backend with 8 new pytest cases (pagination, N+1 guard, public/private visibility) bringing Flask tests to 109 total. 🕷️ Big SEO win!
📋 Open PRs

Add Bash redirection cheatsheet + git-ahead tool — a handy developer-experience addition that rounds out the utility scripts folder. Small but useful! 🔧

The biggest structural change in recent memory is now live on dev:
/api/generate → returns HTTP 202 instantly; Pub/Sub workers process recipes in the background
Workers migrated to Cloud Run HTTP Push endpoints (/api/worker/recipe, /api/worker/image) — true serverless, scale-to-zero, no idle costs

issues.summary: chore: prune stale .claude/worktrees (~1.6 GB) — audit done, cleanup deferred
issues.description:
Prune the stale git worktrees under .claude/worktrees/ (~1.6 GB, 9 worktrees) and decide whether the directory should be gitignored. Deferred from the 2026-07-11 session to a dedicated cleanup session (part of the upcoming repo-wide cleanup/docs planning). Full audit table, per-worktree assessment, and cleanup commands are in GitHub issue #3086:https://github.com/adamtasteslikegood/tasteslikegoodtheangularsvegancookbook/issues/3086
Status at audit time (2026-07-11):
All 9 worktrees have clean working trees.
7 branches fully pushed to origin (safe to remove worktrees; commits survive on remote).
pm-jira-kan-sync must be KEPT while PR #3080 is open.
status-check-2026-06-04: empty (0 commits ahead), removable.
chore+gbrain-railway-claude-md: 2 local-only commits, verified fully superseded by newer CLAUDE.md content on dev — removable.
Re-verify the audit (dirty files / unpushed commits / open PRs) before deleting anything.
[Filed by Claude on Adam's behalf]
